rationale: The value 2.0 (zero fractional part, within dCBOR's D5 reducible range) encoded as an f16 float (0xf9 0x40 0x00) instead of being reduced to the plain integer 2 (0x02). This is distinct from NON_SHORTEST_FLOAT: the float width used (f16) IS already the shortest float form for 2.0 — the defect is that it should not be a float at all. dcbor-only: rfc8949-profile-1 has no numeric-reduction rule (floats and integers are always distinct types there).
